title: Humboltia endotrachys
taxonomy: Humboltia endotrachys is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. This classification places Humboltia endotrachys within the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that also includes coffee, gardenias, and snapdragons. Humboltia endotrachys is further classified as a member of the tribe Plumerieae, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The exact classification of Humboltia endotrachys may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic research. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of this plant species.

intro: Humboldtia endotrachys, also known as the Indian thunder tree or thunder tree, is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the Western Ghats of India. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia endotrachys include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions and infusions, which are traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
